summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a solid ’71 set highlighted by a soaring The Other One, tight Cumberland and tasty Keith on keys.
review: The second set delivers a well‑rehearsed band in top form – Phil Lesh drives the music with an inspired, stretched‑out The Other One, while the group locks into a crisp Cumberland and a swinging Deal. The FLAC soundboard source is clear and faithful, giving listeners a vivid picture of this historic 1971 performance.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise both the musical performance (especially The Other One and Keith's playing) and the clear soundboard recording, indicating an outstanding show with excellent audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note the recording sounds great—crisp, clear FLAC capture with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness. The only limitation mentioned is that it is a short partial set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Even as a partial set, the performance feels tight and energetic; Phil’s leadership lifts the mood after a rough start to the concert, and the transitions between songs feel natural and engaging.

band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for taking control of the music, delivering an inspiring, positive energy that "saved the day" and carrying the band throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is highlighted as being front‑and‑center with tasty, up‑front playing on keyboards (his third show).
